children.text: to identify and understand landslide hazards and risks;
to reduce losses from landslides;
to protect communities at risk of landslide hazards; and
to help improve communication and emergency preparedness, including by coordinating with communities and entities responsible for infrastructure that are at risk of landslide hazards.

children.label: A
children.text: expanding the early warning system for post-wildfire debris flow to include recently burned areas across the western United States;

children.label: C
children.text: identifying high-risk debris flow areas, such as recently burned land and potential lahar hazard areas.

children.heading: Interagency Coordinating Committee on Landslide Hazards
children.label: c Interagency Coordinating Committee on Landslide Hazards

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There is established a committee, to be known as the “Interagency Coordinating Committee on Landslide Hazards”.

children.label: A
children.text: The Secretary, who shall serve as Chairperson of the Committee.

children.label: B
children.text: The Secretary of Agriculture.

children.label: C
children.text: The Secretary of the Army.

children.label: D
children.text: The Secretary of Commerce.

children.label: E
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security.

children.label: F
children.text: The Secretary of Transportation.

children.label: G
children.text: The Director of the National Science Foundation.

children.label: H
children.text: The Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy.

children.label: I
children.text: The Director of the Office of Management and Budget.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 3 Meetings
children.text: The Committee shall meet at the call of the Chairperson.

children.label: A
children.text: advise and oversee the program;

children.label: B
children.text: facilitate communication and coordination across Federal agencies in the planning, management, budgeting, and execution of landslide activities; and

children.label: i
children.text: supporting the development of national goals and priorities for the national strategy;

children.label: ii
children.text: articulating Federal agency roles, responsibilities, and resources for carrying out the national strategy; and

children.label: iii
children.text: overseeing the implementation of the national strategy.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish an advisory committee, to be known as the “Advisory Committee on Landslides” (referred to in this subsection as the “Advisory Committee”).

children.label: A
children.text: of whom none may be an individual described in any of subparagraphs (A) through (F) of ; andsection 7342(a)(1) of title 5

children.heading: Consideration
children.label: B Consideration
children.text: The Secretary and the agency heads described in subparagraphs (B) through (I) of subsection (c)(2) shall take into consideration any recommendation of the Advisory Committee submitted under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Cooperative landslide hazard mapping and assessment program
children.label: 1 Cooperative landslide hazard mapping and assessment program
i
children.text: provide grants, on a competitive basis, to State, territorial, local, and Tribal governments to research, map, assess, and collect data on landslide hazards within the jurisdictions of those governments; and

children.label: ii
children.text: accept and use funds received from other Federal and non-Federal partners to advance the purposes of the program.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall consult annually with the Committee, States, units of local government, territories, and Indian tribes to establish priorities for the grant program under this paragraph.

children.label: I
children.text: that will achieve the greatest landslide hazard and risk reduction;

children.label: II
children.text: that reflect the goals and priorities of the national strategy established under subsection (b)(2)(A);

children.label: III
children.text: not less than 50 percent of the total cost of which is matched by non-Federal sources; and

children.label: IV
children.text: that include acquisition of enhanced elevation data consistent with the 3D Elevation Program.

children.label: i
children.text: the grants; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the findings made from those grants.

children.label: i
children.text: the causes, mechanisms, triggers, hydrology, and geology of landslides;

children.label: ii
children.text: ways to reduce landslide hazards and risks to minimize loss of life and property, including landslide hazard and risk communication, perception, decisionmaking, tools, and technologies; and

children.label: iii
children.text: other goals and priorities of the national strategy established under subsection (b)(2)(A).

children.heading: Eligible entities
children.label: B Eligible entities
children.text: The Director shall determine whether an entity is eligible to receive a grant under this paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: ensure that the grants are provided on a competitive basis;

children.label: ii
children.text: consider grant applications submitted by eligible entities that have developed the application in partnership with 1 or more State geological surveys; and

children.label: I
children.text: the grants; and

children.label: II
children.text: the findings made from those grants.
the goals and accomplishments of the Committee in carrying out the national strategy developed under subsection (b)(2);
the results of the activities of the Committee under this section; and
the extent to which any recommendations of the Advisory Committee under subsection (d)(3)(A) have been implemented.
a description of the landslide event and the implications of the event on communities, including life and property;
recommendations on how the identification of the landslide risk could have been improved prior to the event;
a description of the effectiveness of any warning and risk communication, including the dissemination of warnings by State, territorial, local, and Tribal partners in the affected area;
recommendations to improve risk identification, reduction, and communication to landowners and units of local government;
recommendations to improve landslide hazard preparedness and emergency response activities under this section; and
such other findings as the Secretary determines appropriate.
there is authorized to be appropriated to the United States Geological Survey, $25,000,000 to carry out this section;
there is authorized to be appropriated to the National Science Foundation, $11,000,000 to carry out this section; and
there is authorized to be appropriated to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, $1,000,000 to carry out this section.

children.heading: Derivation of funds
children.label: i Derivation of funds
children.text: Funds to carry out the activities under this section shall be derived from amounts authorized to be appropriated that are enacted after the date of the enactment of this section.
